title: CI-RED-020-B: restore the API Health + Smoke nightly witness (remove api-verify detour)
intent: The scheduled 'API Health + Smoke' workflow has produced no successful witness for many days (persistently cancelled). Root cause is a SCOPE bug, not a timing bug: the workflow named Health + Smoke front-loaded the full `make api-verify` target (check-art-studio-scope + check-boundaries + the broad api-test suite) in a best-effort step, which consumed the entire 20-minute job window and starved the actual smoke witness before it ran. Fix: remove the api-verify detour, make the dedicated smoke harness the behavior-bearing check, and make cancellation impossible-by-construction where it was masking. CI-only; no product/application behavior changes. Follows the tracked dev order + Addendum A in docs/handoffs/.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) .github/workflows/api_health_and_smoke.yml: REMOVED the `Run API health (best-effort)` step that ran `make api-verify` (the full-verification detour). Added a cheap `Preflight (smoke entry points exist)` step (Makefile + api-smoke-posts target + run_api_smoke_posts.sh present). Added a STEP-level `timeout-minutes: 12` to `Run v15.5 smoke (all presets)` — PROVISIONAL, to be replaced by ~2x a measured green-smoke wall-time from a workflow_dispatch run (Addendum item 1); a step timeout converts a hung smoke from ambiguous `cancelled` into a named `failure`. Added an `Assert smoke witness present` step gated `if: !cancelled()` that hard-fails when smoke_posts.json is missing after the smoke step — because every downstream step (badge build) is `if: always()`, this prevents a missing witness from being masked. SPLIT the single job into two: `health-smoke` (NO environment — so a branch workflow_dispatch can actually run the witness; a runner-confirmed environment-protection block on github-pages was rejecting the whole job at start on non-main branches, 0 steps executed) and `pages-deploy` (needs: health-smoke, if: always() && ref==main, carries environment: github-pages). Badges are built in health-smoke from smoke data and handed across the job boundary via an explicit upload-artifact(public_badges)/download-artifact(public_badges) pair; smoke_posts.json is uploaded from health-smoke. Pages deployment is now strictly secondary and cannot gate/cancel the witness. GitHub environment protection is NOT loosened. (2) scripts/smoke/run_api_smoke_posts.sh: hardened the EXISTING `cleanup` EXIT trap. It already preserved the smoke exit code implicitly (no `exit` in body); adding deterministic TERM->wait(~5s)->KILL->reap makes that fragile, so preservation is now EXPLICIT (`rc=$?` first line, `exit "$rc"` last) with every signal/reap `|| true` so cleanup can never flip a real smoke/readiness result. Readiness semantics (router_count>0), endpoint coverage, and Windows guarding are unchanged. (3) scripts/ci/test_api_health_and_smoke_workflow.py (NEW, required guard): text-level policy ratchet asserting no `make api-verify`, still invokes the smoke entry point, still uploads smoke_posts.json, still has a scheduled trigger, AND the smoke step carries a step-level timeout-minutes. (4) docs/handoffs/CI_RED_020B_dev_order.md + CI_RED_020B_addendum.md: tracked dev order + addendum (durable, previously pasted only).
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: No prior guard prevented the full api-verify suite from being front-loaded into a witness workflow, and no step-level timeout existed to make a hung smoke a named failure — so the nightly witness silently degraded to `cancelled` for many days. This removes the detour, converts the failure class cancelled->failure via a measured step timeout, closes the always()-masking hole with an explicit witness-presence gate, and installs a required text-level ratchet so the exact regression (re-adding api-verify or dropping the step timeout) fails CI. The api-verify path itself is untouched where it belongs (Core CI / push+PR).

commands_run: yaml.safe_load(api_health_and_smoke.yml) OK; step order Preflight -> Run v15.5 smoke (timeout-minutes=12) -> Assert smoke witness present (if !cancelled) -> Upload; grep confirms NO `make api-verify` invocation
bash -n scripts/smoke/run_api_smoke_posts.sh -> OK (hardened trap: rc=$? first, exit "$rc" last, TERM/KILL/reap all `|| true`)
services/api venv (py3.11.9): pytest scripts/ci/test_api_health_and_smoke_workflow.py scripts/ci/test_wait_for_api_ready.py -> 21 passed (6 new guard + 15 readiness regression)

PENDING (pre-merge, on the Ubuntu runner): workflow_dispatch on the branch reaches the smoke step, uploads smoke_posts.json, returns terminal (success/failure) NOT cancelled; measure green-smoke wall-time and replace provisional timeout-minutes with ~2x observed (both numbers recorded in PR).

files.intent: Remove the api-verify detour; make the smoke harness the witness; provisional step-timeout (measured pre-merge); witness-presence gate surviving the always() blanket
Harden the existing EXIT trap: explicit exit-code preservation + deterministic TERM->wait->KILL->reap; no readiness/coverage change
files.behavior_change: none - CI smoke harness cleanup only; smoke/readiness result preserved

files.intent: Required text-level ratchet: no api-verify, smoke entry present, artifact uploaded, scheduled trigger, smoke step has step-timeout
Durable tracked copy of the 020-B dev order (previously pasted only)
Addendum A: four pick-up-and-run sharpenings amending the dev order
